summary.quantification_method: Isotopic labeling with light and deuterated heavy formaldehyde

primary_variables.matched_definition: Permanent loss of function through full gene knockout (for example CRISPR KO or deletion).
evidence: quantitatively compare the N-terminal peptides detected in parental and CAPN5-deficient SH-SY5Y neuroblastoma cells
Following inactivation of CAPN5 using the Double Nickase CRISPR method, cells were subjected to single-cell cloning, and a clone with no detectable CAPN5 protein was chosen for further studies
We used TAILS to compare the “N-terminomes” of parental and CAPN5-deficient (CAPN5−/−) SH-SY5Y cells.
The SH-SY5Y cells were transfected with Calpain-5 Double Nickase CRISPR Plasmid (Santa Cruz Biotechnology, Dallas, TX, USA, sc-403881-NIC)

replicate_hints: Our proteomics workflow, based on three biological replicates, is summarized in Figure 1B.
multiplex_channel_hints: The proteins from the parental SH-SY5Y cells were labeled with light formaldehyde (adds 28 Da), whereas the proteins from the CAPN5−/− SH-SY5Y cells were labeled with heavy/deuterated formaldehyde (adds 34 Da).

evidence: quantitatively compare the N-terminal peptides detected in parental and CAPN5-deficient SH-SY5Y neuroblastoma cells

quantification_strategy: Isotopic labeling with light and deuterated heavy formaldehyde

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment explicitly uses formaldehyde isotopic labeling (light vs deuterated heavy) which is the chemical basis for dimethyl labeling, and PRIDE metadata explicitly lists 'Dimethyl Labeling'.
Shotgun proteomics (pre-TAILS) was performed, but the primary quantitative method is dimethyl labeling.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The study explicitly employs the TAILS (Terminal amine isotopic labeling of substrates) N-terminomics approach to identify neo-N-termini.
